focus:
Focus on research that studies large language models (LLMs) through a psychological or cognitive science lens, or that applies psychological frameworks to understand, evaluate, or improve LLM behavior. This includes both empirical investigations of LLM cognition and theoretical work bridging AI and psychology.

Included Content (In Scope)

Cognitive biases, heuristics, and reasoning patterns in LLMs (anchoring, framing, confirmation bias, etc.)
Personality modeling, personality traits, and psychometric evaluation of LLMs
Theory of Mind (ToM), social cognition, and perspective-taking in LLMs
Emotional intelligence, empathy, and affective computing in LLMs
Moral reasoning, ethical decision-making, and value alignment from a psychological perspective
LLM behavior under psychological manipulation (prompt injection, sycophancy, persuasion)
Consistency, self-awareness, and metacognition in LLMs
LLM as participants or simulators in psychological experiments
Comparisons between human and LLM cognitive processes
Benchmarks and evaluation frameworks for psychological or social capabilities of LLMs
Mental health applications involving LLM dialogue (therapy bots, counseling agents)

Excluded Content (Out of Scope)

General NLP or language understanding without psychological framing
Standard LLM benchmarks not focused on cognitive or psychological dimensions
Model pre-training, scaling laws, or architecture improvements without psychological relevance
General alignment or safety work not grounded in psychological theory
Purely sociological or political studies without cognitive focus
